items.title: ChatGPT developer OpenAI said to discuss offering U.S. government a 5% stake: FT
items.description: The proposal aims to give the American public a financial stake in AI while easing political scrutiny of the industry.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin holds above $60,000 as yen jumps on intervention fears
Metaplanet buys another $170 million of bitcoin expanding treasury to 43,000 BTC
items.description: The Japanese firm, now the world's third largest publicly traded bitcoin holder, reported stronger Bitcoin Income Generation revenue.

items.title: Taiko fully restores cross-chain bridge just 10 days after a $1.7 million hack
items.description: The protocol completed a multi-stage recovery that included an independent security review. The TAIKO token has surged as much as 136% in recent trading.

items.title: Solana launches onchain governance and sets entry fee at 100,000 SOL staked
items.description: Validators and the people who stake with them can now formally signal where the network should go through a new system called Solana Governance Proposals. Any validator with 100,000 SOL behind it can open one, and stakers can overrule how their validator votes.

items.title: XRP edges higher as whale activity rises while retail traders stay cautious
items.description: New wallet creation hit a three-month high and large-holder activity strengthened, but XRP still needs to reclaim $1.10 before the recovery looks convincing.

items.title: Bitcoin's long-term holders have returned to accumulation
items.description: Long-term holding wallets have shifted to net accumulation from net distribution, according to Glassnode.

items.title: FBI Director Kash Patel caught sleeping on required disclosure of six-figure MSTR investment: Report
items.description: Patel loaded up on BTC-holder MSTR shares but failed to file a timely disclosure, according to a report by nonpartisan news outlet NOTUS.

items.title: Taiko reopens bridge after $1.7M exploit, says users made whole
items.description: Taiko restored bridge transfers after replenishing asset backing and completing security fixes following an 11-day network disruption.

items.title: Metaplanet buys 2,823 BTC, surpasses 43,000 in Bitcoin holdings
items.description: Metaplanet bought 2,823 Bitcoin during the second quarter, reducing its average acquisition cost to $106,500 per BTC, and reported $10.9 million in revenue from its income generation strategy.

items.title: OpenAI weighs 5% stake for US government amid Trump talks: FT
items.description: OpenAI has reportedly discussed giving the US government a 5% equity stake in early Trump administration talks as Washington tightens AI model oversight.

items.title: Robinhood links with dYdX Labs to launch new DEX Arcus
items.description: The crypto protocol dYdX has rebranded as Arcus and is joining Robinhood’s new blockchain to bring perpetual and tokenized stock trading.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: France to strengthen response as crypto wrench attacks hit 77
items.description: French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez promised a “more ambitious” three-part plan to reinforce security measures for the crypto sector.

items.title: Dubai crypto market hits 50 licensed firms after new VARA approval
items.description: Dubai’s VARA has licensed its 50th crypto firm, though only 39 VASPs were fully operational at the end of 2025 as the emirate’s regulated market continues to expand.

items.title: Analyst warns BTC could drop further after worst June since 2022
items.description: The June close above realized price but below the 200-week moving average “signals the bear bottom is still ahead per prior cycles,” one analyst said.

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
items.description: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

items.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
items.description: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

items.title: Nvidia offers start-up customers chance to swap compute power for revenue share
items.description: Nvidia says it will give start-ups the chance to swap access to compute power for a slice of future profits under a new program aimed at AI-focused firms.

items.title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
items.description: The Russian defence ministry said that it launched a "massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones."

items.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
items.description: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

items.title: Apple plans five new iPhones through 2027, eyes Chinese-made chips amid foldable push, reports say
items.description: Apple is planning five new iPhones by 2027 and is considering Chinese memory suppliers as it expands foldable production, reports say.

items.title: Automakers report mixed U.S. sales results as hybrid vehicles drive market
items.description: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without them.

items.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
items.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

items.title: This is how we can make AI safe for everyone
items.description: The labs develop the technology, but citizens and their elected representatives must make the rules

items.title: White House accelerates plans for AI model standards
items.description: Guidance to be announced as soon as next week after government intervention in Anthropic and OpenAI rollouts

items.title: Trump’s billion-dollar makeover of Washington DC
items.description: The US president calls it a ‘gift’ to the nation. Critics say he is racing past checks to stamp his mark on the capital

items.title: The economics of women’s weight
items.description: A new study suggests weight-loss drugs help women find work — and prompt men to leave their partners

items.title: What ice cream pricing tells us about Japan
items.description: The food industry is a shopfront for the vulnerabilities of a weak currency and an economy dependent on imports

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment of Kyiv
items.description: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: Russian drone campaign mapped Nato air defence gaps, study finds
items.description: Nearly 150 UAV incursions into more than a dozen European countries were made over 19 months, IISS report says

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mut3NTl36xc! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: ‘There was a catch’: My doctor friend was outraged by his hospital’s fundraising tactics. Where do we draw the line?
items.description: “This shocking program was exploiting doctors and the trust inherent in their relationships with patients.”

items.title: Warsh’s throwaway comment injects life into the debasement trade, for one day at least
items.description: As much as new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh tried to refrain from steering markets, it looks like he did.

items.title: Tamera Mowry lists Bay Area house for $2.7 million as she prepares to build Napa Valley ‘dream home’
items.description: “Sister, Sister” star Tamera Mowry-Housley has listed her longtime California house for $2.7 million—months after revealing that she and her husband are in the process of building a new home in Napa Valley.

items.title: After eight years, Google loses final appeal against $4.7 billion European Union fine
items.description: The Court of Justice of the European Union ruled to uphold its fine against Google over alleged anti-competition practices

items.title: OpenAI reportedly considers handing 5% stake to the government. Here’s why the AI lab would consider that move.
items.description: Bringing the government on board as an equity partner may serve to dilute some of the criticism of the AI industry that has developed momentum in recent months.

items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.description: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
items.description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: Nvidia is betting on a trillion-dollar robotics boom. Here is the hidden way to trade it.
items.description: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

items.title: FirstFT: OpenAI proposes US government stake
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Asia’s biggest IPO this year, and Google loses appeal against record €4bn EU competition fine

items.title: What ice cream pricing tells us about Japan
items.description: The food industry is a shopfront for the vulnerabilities of a weak currency and an economy dependent on imports

items.title: How AI could make markets worse
items.description: Heed the lessons of information economics

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment of Kyiv
items.description: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: Beijing plane crash pilot suffered from ‘insomnia and anxiety’, say police
items.description: Pilot who was living alone after divorce had made ‘repeated references’ to suicide in his diary, according to authorities

items.title: Trump blocks long-term renewal of North America trade pact
items.description: Washington will hold annual reviews on terms of commerce with its biggest trading partners

items.title: What Hammersmith Bridge could teach Burnham
items.description: Devolved authorities do not face right or wrong choices, only trade-offs

items.title: Russian drone campaign mapped Nato air defence gaps, study finds
items.description: Nearly 150 UAV incursions into more than a dozen European countries were made over 19 months, IISS report says

items.title: A.I. Is Reshaping the Economy. Good Luck Measuring How.
items.description: Some data suggest artificial intelligence is already causing job losses. Other sources show the opposite. Why is it so hard to figure out what’s going on?

items.title: Jaguar’s Electric Future: Curves Are Out, and Blunt Is In
items.description: The storied British automaker is going all-electric, and this fall it will unveil its new flagship, with deliveries coming next year. The strategy is distinctive but risky and has drawn some prominent detractors.

items.title: Chanel Is Buying Charvet, the World’s Oldest Shirt Maker
items.description: The French luxury group is acquiring Charvet, a Parisian brand that dates to the 1830s, in a bid to add some men’s wear savoir-faire to its portfolio.

items.title: Trump Administration Delivers Lucrative Win for Its Kratom Allies
items.description: In moving to ban a potent synthetic version of kratom, the president’s team paved the way for more sales for makers of rival botanic supplements, who had aggressively lobbied for the change.

items.title: Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
items.description: President Trump and his family reaped vast financial rewards from a memecoin that generated losses for hundreds of thousands of investors.

items.title: Who Can Sell ‘6 7’ Chicken Nuggets? One Company Is Asking a Court to Decide.
items.description: The meat and poultry giant Perdue said in a lawsuit that Soules Foods copied its packaging that is based on a meme popular with children.

items.title: Charles H. Townsend Dies at 82; Led Condé Nast During Digital Transition
items.description: By 2016, when he retired from the parent company of glossy magazines like Vogue, Vanity Fair and The New Yorker, print was seriously struggling.

items.title: Trump Accounts to Receive $250 Million Donation From Chip Maker Micron
items.description: President Trump said the U.S. chip maker would make a significant donation to a new type of investment account created by the administration.
